title:
feat(algom-alpha): engagement bot + X filtered stream for prospect monitoring

- engagement-bot.js: auto-reply to mentions + auto-follow substantive commenters
  * Fetches recent mentions on @reviceva every 45min
  * Groq/Claude Haiku generates contextual replies (max 2 per run)
  * Auto-follows users who left substantive comments (max 3 per run)
  * State tracked in engagement_state.json — no double-replies within 48h

- stream-listener.js: real-time X filtered stream monitoring
  * Keywords: 'fractional CTO', 'need CTO', 'AI engineer hiring', 'HubSpot CRM pain'
  * Auto-like + optional follow on matching tweets
  * Max 15 actions/hour — rate limit safe
  * State tracked in stream_state.json for dedup

- index.js: wired both modules into Algom Alpha startup sequence
  * Engagement loop: every 45min, max 2 replies + 3 follows
  * Filtered stream: starts with autoLike + autoFollow enabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title:
feat(x-webhook): X Activity API full automation — auto-follow back + auto-DM reply

- X Activity API v2 webhook live at webhook.aideazz.xyz/cto/x-webhook
  * CRC challenge handler (HMAC-SHA256) — webhook verified by X
  * Signature verification on all POST events
  * Fixed body parsing: express.json verify callback saves rawBody before middleware consumes it

- Account Activity API subscription created (OAuth 1.0a) — ALL account events flowing:
  * Follow/Unfollow, DM received, Mentions, Likes, Profile changes
  * Separate profile event subscriptions via X Activity API console (Bio/Pic/Screenname)

- Auto-follow back: when @reviceva gets a new follower → auto-follows back via v2.follow()
- Auto-DM reply: incoming DMs → Claude Haiku generates contextual reply → v2 DM endpoint
  * DM sending blocked at PPU tier (X API 403) — auto-reply queued for Basic tier upgrade
- Telegram broadcast: Follow/DM/Mention events → @aitcf_aideazz_bot → Elena's personal Telegram

- Added twitter-api-v2 dependency (OAuth 1.0a client)
- Added TWITTER_API_KEY/SECRET/ACCESS_TOKEN/SECRET to .env for CTO AIPA process
- Fixed Elena's Twitter user ID: 1563632998863577092 (was wrong 30551469 placeholder)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat: HubSpot CRM + AI prospecting engine live
description: AIdeazz now has a full CRM integration. HubSpot receives leads from Hacker News, GitHub, and Product Hunt automatically. Claude Haiku classifies pain points and pushes verified contacts into HubSpot as Contacts, Companies and Deals. Fresh prospects flow in every Tuesday and Friday at zero cost on Oracle Always-Free.

title:
feat: multi-source fresh leads engine — HN Who is Hiring + GitHub + Product Hunt

- fresh-leads-ingest.ts: new file, three live sources
  * Hacker News 'Ask HN: Who is Hiring?' — 200 companies/month, parsed via Algolia API
  * GitHub repo search — repos with AI/automation + contact email in README
  * Product Hunt AI launches — optional, needs PRODUCT_HUNT_TOKEN
  * Dedup vs Oracle, Claude Haiku pain-point classification, HubSpot push for real emails

- telegram-bot.ts:
  * /fresh_leads command — manual trigger (HN+GH by default, /fresh_leads all adds PH)
  * Cron: Tue + Fri 7 AM Panama — automatic twice-weekly fresh pull
  * Added to outreach menu

title:
feat: HubSpot CRM integration — auto-push leads from triage + prospect ingestion

- hubspot-client.ts: full HubSpot CRM API v3 wrapper
  • upsertContact / upsertCompany / createDeal with upsert logic
  • v4 associations API (contact↔company, deal↔contact, deal↔company)
  • addNoteToContact (pain point + matched system)
  • pushLeadToHubSpot() — full pipeline in one call
  • getHubSpotStats() — live contact/company/deal counts
  • HS_STAGES map for default pipeline stage IDs

- prospect-ingest.ts: push each YC prospect to HubSpot after Oracle import
- lead-triage.ts: push client_lead/partnership signals (urgency ≥ 3) to HubSpot
  • urgency 4-5 → stage engaged; urgency 3 → stage contacted
- telegram-bot.ts: /hubspot command shows live CRM stats; added to menu

title:
feat(trello-voice): Tier 2 — subtask decomposition + smarter urgency

Subtask decomposition:
- NLP detects multi-step tasks and returns subtasks[] when >= 2 distinct
  steps are explicitly enumerated (e.g. 'prepare demo: design slides,
  set up env, send invites' → 3 cards)
- Each subtask gets same board/list/label/dueDate as the parent
- Reply shows numbered list of created cards with links
- Atomic tasks (single action) stay as one card — null subtasks

Smarter urgency (reconcileUrgency):
- dueDate today/tomorrow → listTarget forced to just_for_today
- dueDate set → listTarget corrected to dated (if not already urgent)
- listTarget 'dated' with no extracted date → corrected to todo_flow
- Ensures list and date never contradict each other
description:
feat(trello-voice): Tier 2 — subtask decomposition + smarter urgency

Subtask decomposition:
- NLP detects multi-step tasks and returns subtasks[] when >= 2 distinct
  steps are explicitly enumerated (e.g. 'prepare demo: design slides,
  set up env, send invites' → 3 cards)
- Each subtask gets same board/list/label/dueDate as the parent
- Reply shows numbered list of created cards with links
- Atomic tasks (single action) stay as one card — null subtasks

Smarter urgency (reconcileUrgency):
- dueDate today/tomorrow → listTarget forced to just_for_today
- dueDate set → listTarget corrected to dated (if not already urgent)
- listTarget 'dated' with no extracted date → corrected to todo_flow
- Ensures list and date never contradict each other

title:
feat(trello-voice): Tier 1 — due-date extraction + duplicate card detection

Due dates:
- NLP prompt extracts 'by end of May', 'by Friday', 'tomorrow' etc.
- dueDate (YYYY-MM-DD) added to CardClassification and passed to Trello API
- Displayed as 'Due: May 31' in Telegram reply

Duplicate detection:
- Before creating, fetches existing cards on the target list
- titleSimilarity() checks word-overlap >= 50%
- If duplicate found: skips creation, replies with link to existing card
- Non-fatal: if check fails, creation proceeds normally
description:
feat(trello-voice): Tier 1 — due-date extraction + duplicate card detection

Due dates:
- NLP prompt extracts 'by end of May', 'by Friday', 'tomorrow' etc.
- dueDate (YYYY-MM-DD) added to CardClassification and passed to Trello API
- Displayed as 'Due: May 31' in Telegram reply

Duplicate detection:
- Before creating, fetches existing cards on the target list
- titleSimilarity() checks word-overlap >= 50%
- If duplicate found: skips creation, replies with link to existing card
- Non-fatal: if check fails, creation proceeds normally

title:
fix(x-tech-updater): use Claude/Groq to write human-readable tweets

Replace raw commit message formatting with an AI-generated post that
explains the feature in plain language for a public audience. Fallback
chain: Claude Haiku → Groq → plain text (with commit syntax stripped).
